verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein predominantly resident in the ER membrane, where its N-terminal EF-hand/SAM domain faces the ER lumen and its cytoplasmic C-terminus activates Orai1 channels. However, a well-documented minority pool of STIM1 (STIM1PM) localizes to the plasma membrane with its N-terminal domain extracellular and accessible — confirmed by flow cytometry on live intact cells (Jurkat, RBL), surface biotinylation, and extracellular-epitope antibodies. This PM pool has functional roles in constitutive Ca2+ entry and ARC channel regulation. The dominant compartment is ER, not PM, placing this as contextual/dual_localization rather than yes.
